annotations.label: 1 is the first natural number after 0

anchor.snippet: The number 1 is the first natural number after

annotations.note: The natural number 1 is definitionally Nat.succ 0, the successor of 0 in the (core Lean) inductive type ℕ that Mathlib builds on.

annotations.label: Construction by succession

anchor.snippet: is constructed by succession

annotations.note: ℕ is the inductive type generated from 0 by the successor constructor Nat.succ, exactly the construction-by-succession described.

annotations.label: 1 is neither prime nor composite

anchor.snippet: it is regarded as neither a prime nor a composite number

annotations.note: Nat.not_prime_one formalizes ¬Prime 1, but Mathlib has no dedicated 'composite' predicate to capture the full neither-prime-nor-composite claim.

annotations.label: Multiplicative identity

anchor.snippet: is the multiplicative identity of the integers

annotations.note: one_mul/mul_one state 1 is the multiplicative identity in any MulOneClass/monoid, which includes ℤ as a special case.

annotations.label: Powers of 1 equal 1

anchor.snippet: any other power of 1 is always equal to 1 itself

annotations.note: one_pow states (1 : M) ^ n = 1 for all n in any monoid, exactly matching the claim.

annotations.label: Multiplicative identity in a unital ring or field

anchor.snippet: it denotes the multiplicative identity in any unital ring or field

annotations.note: MulOneClass axiomatizes 1 with one_mul/mul_one as the two-sided multiplicative identity, generalizing the unital-ring/field case.

annotations.label: Unit (element with multiplicative inverse)

anchor.snippet: An element with a multiplicative inverse is called a unit

annotations.note: IsUnit a is defined as a being in the range of the coercion from Units, i.e. having a two-sided multiplicative inverse.

annotations.label: 1 as identity for power semigroup

anchor.snippet: so that 1 is also the identity for any power semigroup

annotations.note: Mathlib's pointwise Set monoid (Set.mulOneClass) has {1} as identity, but this is not phrased as the 'power semigroup' identity statement.

annotations.label: 1 is its own factorial

anchor.snippet: 1 is its own factorial

annotations.note: Nat.factorial_one states 1! = 1, exactly the claim that 1 is its own factorial.

annotations.label: Empty product is 1

anchor.snippet: the empty product , that is the product of a set of zero numbers, is also 1

annotations.note: Finset.prod_empty states the product over the empty Finset equals 1.

annotations.label: Peano axioms starting point

anchor.snippet: 1 was treated as the starting point of the sequence of natural numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's ℕ is the inductive (Peano) naturals but starts at 0 with no explicit Peano-axiom structure designating 1 as the starting point (PNat starts at 1 but is unrelated to the historical axiomatization).

annotations.label: Von Neumann construction of 1

anchor.snippet: a set containing only the element 0

annotations.note: Mathlib defines ordinals via well-orders and has a von Neumann cumulative hierarchy (ZFSet.vonNeumann), but does not construct the natural number 1 as the set {0}.

annotations.label: Unary numeral system

anchor.snippet: The unary numeral system , as used in tallying

annotations.note: Nat.unaryCast builds a value by repeated +1 (the unary representation of n), the closest analog, but there is no general unary numeral system.

annotations.label: Unit interval

anchor.snippet: normalized to fall within the unit interval

annotations.note: unitInterval is defined as Set.Icc (0:ℝ) 1, the unit interval.

annotations.label: 1 as probability of a certain event

anchor.snippet: 1 is the probability of an event that is absolutely or almost certain to occur

annotations.note: IsProbabilityMeasure requires measure_univ : μ univ = 1, i.e. the certain event (whole space) has probability 1.

annotations.label: Unit vectors

anchor.snippet: vectors are often normalized into unit vectors

annotations.note: norm_smul_inv_norm shows ‖(‖x‖⁻¹) • x‖ = 1, i.e. normalization yields a norm-1 vector, but there is no dedicated 'unit vector' type/definition.

annotations.label: Benford's law

anchor.snippet: which states that the probability for a specific leading digit

annotations.note: No grep hits for Benford's law in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Legendre's constant

anchor.snippet: 1 is the value of Legendre's constant

annotations.note: No grep hits for Legendre's constant in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weil's conjecture on Tamagawa numbers

anchor.snippet: The Weil's conjecture on Tamagawa numbers states that the Tamagawa number

annotations.note: No grep hits for Tamagawa numbers in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Church numeral for 1

anchor.snippet: the Church numeral for 1 is represented by the function

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize Church numerals / lambda-calculus encodings of natural numbers.
